title: Tapping to Keep Faith Through Market Fluctuations
subtitle: A tapping practice for staying steady when financial numbers rise and fall beyond your control
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 68 of a 12-week money program. The script targets the anxiety that comes when markets, investments, or income varies — the urge to check accounts too often, the fear of a crash undoing what has been built. The reframe moves toward a long-term view grounded in trust rather than daily numbers.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 68 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use when checking accounts or financial news more than once a day has become a habit, or when a dip in investments, income, or savings has triggered anxiety about the long-term picture.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 68 of a 12-week money program. The script targets the anxiety that comes when external financial numbers vary and the nervous system interprets short-term movement as permanent threat — markets dipping, income fluctuating, a savings balance moving the wrong way. The reframe builds a long-term view: wealth expanding over years, not days, and the ability to stay calm through the cycles.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though markets rise and fall, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I've worried about downturns, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I feared losing what I built, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I've felt anxious when the numbers dip.
Side of eye: I've checked accounts too often.
Under eye: I feared a crash could undo what I've built.
Under nose: I felt unsteady in financial uncertainty.
Chin: I'm ready to stay calm whatever the market does.
Collarbone: I trust long-term growth over short-term movement.
Under arm: I relax as cycles come and go.
Top of head: I know my wealth isn't tied to today's swings.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I trust wealth beyond daily market moods.
Side of eye: I stay steady through highs and lows.
Under eye: My assets keep growing over time.
Under nose: I hold a calm, long view.
Chin: I let fluctuations build future gains.
Collarbone: I believe in expansion across the years.
Under arm: I rest easy whatever today's numbers show.
Top of head: I keep deep faith in lasting abundance.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from Day 68 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
